metadata.title: Petroleum Inventory Trajectories Under the 2026 Strait of Hormuz Closure: A Mass-Balance Forecast Model with Sensitivity Analysis and Falsifiable Predictions

cuerpo.resumen: This paper develops a weekly mass-balance forecasting model for U.S. petroleum inventories during the 2026 Strait of Hormuz closure crisis and applies it to identify the calendar dates at which Strategic Petroleum Reserve, commercial crude oil, motor gasoline, and distillate fuel oil stocks are forecast to cross their respective operational floors...

secciones.contenido: On 28 February 2026, the United States and Israel initiated a coordinated air campaign against the Islamic Republic of Iran. Within hours, Iran responded by mining the Strait of Hormuz... The central question of this paper is straightforward: under continuation of currently observable flow rates, when do U.S. petroleum inventories cross the operational floors?

The Strait of Hormuz lies between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman... As of May 1, 2026, the SPR held approximately 392 million barrels... Commercial crude oil inventories stood at 457.2 million barrels, motor gasoline at 228 million barrels, and distillate fuel at 104 million barrels.
All flow and stock data used in this paper are drawn from publicly available U.S. government sources (EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, STEO, and DOE).

r_markdown_componentes.tabla_fuentes:
```{r tabla-fuentes, echo=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

fuentes_data <- data.frame(
  Fuente = c('EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report', 'EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook', 'U.S. DOE / Office of Petroleum Reserves', 'Industry analyst commentary'),
  Variables = c('Crude production, stocks, flows', '2026 production, price forecast', 'SPR inventory & release schedule', 'Operational floor estimates'),
  Frecuencia = c('Weekly', 'Monthly', 'Weekly', 'Various'),
  Cutoff = c('May 1, 2026', 'April 7, 2026', 'May 1, 2026', 'Q1-Q2 2026')
)

kable(fuentes_data, caption = 'Data sources used in the model.') %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c('striped', 'hover', 'condensed'))
```

secciones.contenido: The model is a deterministic, weekly-resolution mass-balance accounting framework. Stocks are tracked for four buckets: SPR, commercial crude, gasoline, and distillate.

r_markdown_componentes.grafico_estacionalidad:
```{r grafico-estacionalidad, fig.cap='Seasonal demand multiplier for motor gasoline (2026)', echo=FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
library(ggplot2)

estacionalidad <- data.frame(
  Semana = c(1, 4, 7, 9, 12, 16),
  Fecha = c('May 8', 'May 29', 'June 19', 'July 3', 'July 24', 'August 21'),
  Multiplier = c(1.000, 1.040, 1.065, 1.080, 1.065, 1.030)
)

ggplot(estacionalidad, aes(x = Semana, y = Multiplier)) +
  geom_line(color = '#104e8b', size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = '#e41a1c', size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Fecha), vjust = -1, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.98, 1.10)) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = 'sans') +
  labs(x = 'Forecast Week (t)', y = expression(sigma(t)), title = 'Gasoline Seasonal Multiplier Path') +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), plot.title = element_text(face = 'bold'))
```

secciones.contenido: The baseline forecast holds all flow parameters at their week-ending May 1, 2026 values.
